29.19: Aservant cannot be taught by words: because he understandeth that which thou sayst, and contemneth to answer.
29.20: Hast thou seen a man swift to speak? Folly is rather to be hoped, than his amendment.
29.21: He that nourished his servant delicately from his childhood, afterward shall feel him stubborn.
29.22: An angry man provoketh brawls: and he that is easy to indignation, shall be more prone to sin.
29.23: Humiliation followeth the proud: and glory shall receive the humble of spirit.
29.24: He that is partaker with a thief, hateth his own soul: he heareth one adjuring, and telleth not.
29.25: He that feareth man, shall soon fall: he that trusteth in our Lord shall be lifted up.
29.26: Many seek after the face of the Prince: & the judgement of every one cometh forth from our Lord.
29.27: The just abhor an impious man: & the impious abhor them that are in the right way. The son that keepeth the word, shall be out of perdition.

30.1: The words of the Gatherer the son of Vomiter. The vision, that the man spake, with whom God is, and who being strengthened by God abiding with him, said:
30.2: I am most foolish of men, & the wisdom of men is not with me.
30.3: I have not learned wisdom, and have not known the science of saints.
30.4: Who hath ascended into heaven and descended? Who hath contained the spirit in his hands? Who hath bound the waters together as in a garment? Who hath raised up all the borders of the earth? What is his name, and what is the name of his son, if thou know?
30.5: Every word of God tried by fire, is a buckler to them that hope in him:
30.6: Add not any thing to his words, and so thou be reproved and found a liar.
30.7: Two things I have asked thee, deny them not to me before I die.
30.8: Vanity, and lying words make far from me. Beggary, and riches give me not: give only things necessary for my sustenance:
30.9: lest perhaps being filled I be allured to deny, and may say: Who is the Lord? Or being compelled by poverty I may steal, and forswear the name of my God.
30.10: Accuse not a servant to his master, lest perhaps he curse thee, and thou fall.
30.11: There is a generation that curseth their father, and that blesseth not their mother.
30.12: Ageneration, that seemeth to it-self clean, & yet is not washed from their filthiness.
30.13: Ageneration, whose eyes are lofty, and the eye-lids thereof set up on high.
30.14: Ageneration, that for teeth hath swords, and chaweth with their grinding teeth, that they may eat the needy out of the earth, and the poor from among men.
30.15: The horseleech hath two daughters that say: Bring, bring. Three things are unsatiable, the fourth never saith it sufficeth.
30.16: Hell, and the mouth of the matrice, & the earth which is not satisfied with water: but the fire never saith it sufficeth.
30.17: The eye that scorneth his father, & that despiseth the travail of his mother in bearing him, let the ravens of the torrents pick it out, and the young of the eagle eat it.
30.18: Three things are hard to me, and of the fourth I am utterly ignorant.
30.19: The way of an eagle in the air, the way of a serpent upon a rock, the way of a ship in the midst of the sea, and the way of a man in youth.
30.20: Such is also the way of an adulterous woman, which eateth, and wiping her mouth saith: I have done no evil.
30.21: By three things the earth is moved, and the fourth it cannot sustain.
30.22: By a servant when he shall reign: by a fool when he shall be filled with meat:
30.23: by an odious woman when she shall be taken in matrimony: & by a bond-woman when she shall be heir to her mistress.
30.24: There are four the least things of the earth, and they are wiser than the wise.
30.25: The ants, a weak people, which prepareth in the harvest meat for themselves:
30.26: The leveret, a people not strong, which placeth his bed in the rock:
30.27: The locust hath no king, and they go out all by their troops:
30.28: The stellion stayeth on his hands, & tarrieth in king's houses.
30.29: There are three things which go well, and the fourth that goeth happily.
30.30: The lion, the strongest of beasts shall fear at the meeting of none:
30.31: the cock girded about the loins, and the ram: also the king, against whom none can resist.
30.32: There is that hath appeared a fool after that he was lifted up on high: for if he had understood, he would have laid his hand upon his mouth.
30.33: And he that strongly presseth the paps to wring out milk, straineth out butter, and he that violently cleanseth his nose, wringeth out blood: & he that provoketh angers, bringeth forth discords.

31.1: The words of Lamuel the king. The vision wherewith his mother instructed him.
31.2: What o my beloved, what o the beloved of my womb, what o beloved of my vows?
31.3: Give not thy substance to women, & thy riches to destroy kings.
31.4: Give not to kings, o Lamuel, give not wine to kings: because there is no secret where drunkenness reigneth,
31.5: & lest perhaps they drink, & forget judgments, & change the cause of the children of the poor.
31.6: Give strong drink to them that be sad, and wine unto them that are of a pensive mind:
31.7: let them drink, and forget their poverty, and not remember their sorrow any more.
31.8: Open thy mouth to the dumb, & to the causes of all the children that pass:
31.9: open thy mouth, decree that which is just, & judge the needy & poor.
31.10: Avaliant woman who shall find? Far, and from the utmost borders is the price of her.
31.11: The heart of her husband trusteth in her, and he shall not need spoils.
31.12: She shall render good, and not evil, all the days of her life.
31.13: She hath sought wool and flax, and hath wrought by the counsel of her hands.
31.14: She is become as a merchant's ship, bringing her bread from far.
31.15: And she hath risen in the night, and given prey to her household, and meats to her handmaids.
31.16: She hath viewed a field, and bought it, of the fruit of her hands she hath planted a vineyard.
31.17: She hath girded her loins with strength, and hath strengthened her arm.
31.18: She hath tasted, and seen that her traffic is good: her lamp shall not be extinguished in the night.
31.19: She hath put her hand to strong things, and her fingers have taken hold of the spindle.
31.20: She hath opened her hand to the needy, and stretched out her palms to the poor.
31.21: She shall not fear for her house in the colds of snow: for all her household are clothed with double.
31.22: Tapestry clothing she hath made to herself: silk and purple is her garment.
31.23: Her husband is noble in the gates, when he shall sit with the senators of the land.
31.24: She made sindon, and sold it, and delivered a girdle to the Chananeite.
31.25: Strength and beauty is her garment, and she shall laugh in the later day.
31.26: She hath opened her mouth to wisdom, and the law of clemency is in her tongue.
31.27: She hath considered the paths of her house, and hath not eaten her bread idle.
31.28: Her children arose, and commended her to be most blessed: her husband, and he praised her.
31.29: Many daughters have gathered together riches: thou hast passed them all.
31.30: Grace is deceitful, and beauty is vain: the woman that feareth our Lord shall be praised.
31.31: Give ye to her of the fruit of her hands, and let her works praise her in the gates.
